removal: Review and remove these exceptions during the migration-aware deeper rename.

pathPatterns.reason: The policy must name the former product in order to explain each exception.
Historical product requirements explicitly document the former working names.
Archived engineering records preserve the terminology used when written.
Dated audit evidence is an immutable historical record.
Generated historical specifications preserve their original project identity.
The rename plan must name the former product to define its scope and exclusions.

linePatterns.pattern: Guide Your Flock Of Agents|Guide your flock of agents
linePatterns.reason: The canonical Shepherd tagline intentionally uses Flock as a collective noun for the user's agents.

linePatterns.pattern: previously named Flock|Older docs may say .Flock
linePatterns.reason: The public transition notice names the former product once so retained technical identifiers are not confusing.

linePatterns.pattern: Renamed the user-visible product from Flock
linePatterns.reason: The unreleased changelog records the old and new product names for upgrade clarity.
Installed systemd-unit compatibility marker retained for existing nodes.
Tests deliberately use Flock as user-owned project data to prove the rename does not mutate user content.
